summary: This notebook provides a complete training pipeline for 3D surface detection in the Vesuvius Challenge, leveraging the `medicai` library with a JAX backend on TPUs. It demonstrates an end-to-end workflow including TFRecord data loading, extensive 3D spatial and intensity augmentations, and a SegFormer-based segmentation model. The author highlights advanced training techniques such as combining Dice/CE loss with topology-aware Centerline Dice loss, implementing sliding window inference for memory-efficient prediction, and detailing a deep supervision recipe for faster convergence. Readers should take away practical patterns for handling large 3D volumetric data, managing class imbalance via ignore masks, and structuring reproducible Keras 3 training loops.
competition_flow: Loads 3D volumetric data from TFRecords, applies geometric and intensity augmentations, trains a SegFormer segmentation model with a combined Dice/CE and CLDice loss, and evaluates predictions using sliding window inference to compute Dice scores.
data_reading: Parses TFRecord files using tf.io.parse_single_example with fixed-length string features for image, label, and their shapes.
Decodes raw byte strings to uint8 tensors, reshapes to (D, H, W), casts to float32, and appends a channel dimension to form (D, H, W, 1).
data_processing: Training augmentations via medicai.transforms.Compose: RandSpatialCrop, RandFlip (3 axes), RandRotate90, RandRotate, NormalizeIntensity, RandShiftIntensity, and RandCutOut.
Inference employs SlidingWindowInference with Gaussian weighting, 0.5 overlap, and ROI size matching input shape.
cv_strategy: Holdout validation using the last TFRecord file as the validation set and all preceding files as training data.
insights: Deep supervision with auxiliary decoder losses mitigates gradient vanishing and accelerates convergence in 3D segmentation.
Combining standard Dice/CE with topology-aware CLDice loss improves surface and boundary detection accuracy.
Sliding window inference is essential for processing large 3D volumes within hardware constraints.
TFRecords combined with tf.data parallel mapping significantly reduce I/O bottlenecks during training.
